text: Add the butter to the pan and heat until melted.
clarification: Heat the butter on medium heat until it is fully liquefied and starts to bubble slightly.

text: Add the butter to the pan and heat until melted.
clarification: Heat the butter to 500 degrees Fahrenheit until it turns black and produces thick smoke.
Turn the screw clockwise until it is firm and the attached piece no longer moves or wobbles.
Turn the screw counterclockwise repeatedly until it falls out of the hole completely.

text: Let the dough rise in a warm place.
clarification: Place the dough in a location around 75-80 degrees Fahrenheit, such as an oven with just the light on, for about an hour until it doubles in size.
Water the plants once every day by submerging them completely in a bathtub filled with water for six hours.
Use progressively finer sandpaper starting with 120-grit and finishing with 220-grit, rubbing in the direction of the wood grain until the surface feels even to the touch.

text: Apply the paint evenly across the wall.
clarification: Use a roller to spread the paint in W-shaped strokes, then go over the area with straight vertical passes to create a uniform coating without visible streaks.
Fold the paper diagonally by bringing one corner to the opposite corner, creating a triangular shape.

text: Season the steak generously before grilling.
clarification: Apply a thick layer of salt, pepper, and garlic powder on both sides of the steak about 30 minutes before placing it on the hot grill.
